issues.text: Whether there has been a violation of Article 10 of the Convention in that the applicant was punished and considered to have violated her professional duties for making statements to a newspaper

anchor.surface_text: The applicant complains of a violation of Article 10 (Art. 10) of the Convention in that she was punished and was considered to have violated her professional duties for making statements to a newspaper (application No. 22947/93).

issues.text: Whether there has been a violation of Article 2 of the Convention due to failure to protect the applicant's husband's right to life, tacit authorisation of killings, failure to investigate, and administrative practice

anchor.surface_text: As to Article 2, the applicant alleges that the Turkish State failed to provide sufficient protection of her husband's right to life. She refers to the high incidence and systematic pattern of killings of Kurdish teachers...

issues.text: Whether there has been a violation of Article 6 of the Convention due to failure to investigate the murder preventing pursuit of a civil claim for compensation

anchor.surface_text: As to Article 6, the applicant submits that, because of the failure adequately to investigate the circumstances of her husband's murder, she has been unable to pursue her claim for compensation due to her through the civil courts.

issues.text: Whether there has been a violation of Article 13 of the Convention due to lack of independent national authority to investigate and prosecute those responsible for killings

anchor.surface_text: As to Article 13, the applicant complains that there is no independent national authority in a position to investigate and bring to justice those responsible for this kind of killing.

issues.text: Whether there has been a violation of Article 14 in conjunction with Articles 2 and 13 due to discrimination against Turkish citizens of Kurdish origin in protection of life and effective remedies

anchor.surface_text: As to Article 14 in conjunction with Articles 2 and 13, the applicant submits that Turkish citizens of Kurdish origin are the targets and/or victims of the breakdown of protection of the right to life and of the failure to provide effective remedies far more frequently than are other Turkish citizens.

issues.text: Whether there has been a violation of Article 1 of Protocol No. 1 due to refusal of compensation and widow's pension because the killing was by unknown perpetrators

anchor.surface_text: As to Article 1 of Protocol No. 1, the applicant complains that, as a result of the finding that her husband had been killed by unknown perpetrators, she did not receive any compensation or any widow's pension.

arguments.claim: The applications cannot be declared inadmissible for failure to exhaust domestic remedies because the case was communicated to the Government, they submitted no observations raising the issue, and it is the Commission's normal practice not to declare inadmissible in such circumstances unless raised by the Government
